values.value:
== QUEUE RULES == File path: queues/QueueApiName.queue metadataType: Queue memberName: QueueApiName
CRITICAL — ELEMENT ORDER (Salesforce rejects wrong order): <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <Queue xmlns="http://soap.sforce.com/2006/04/metadata"> <doesSendEmailToMembers>false</doesSendEmailToMembers> <!-- email: include ONLY when explicitly provided --> <email>queue@example.com</email> <name>Queue Display Name</name> <queueMembers> <!-- Include <users> only when explicit real usernames are provided in ticket --> </queueMembers> <queueRoutingConfig>RoutingConfigApiName</queueRoutingConfig> <queueSobject> <sobjectType>Case</sobjectType> </queueSobject> </Queue>
MANDATORY ELEMENT ORDER: 1. doesSendEmailToMembers 2. email (optional — omit entirely if not provided) 3. name 4. queueMembers (optional — omit entirely if no members) 5. queueRoutingConfig (optional — omit entirely if not provided) 6. queueSobject (one block per object — at least one required)
CRITICAL — QUEUE MEMBER RULE: <user> tags MUST only contain usernames explicitly stated in the ticket in email format. If ticket says "Sales Users", "Support Users", "Team Members" or any non-email description: → NEVER convert them to email format → OMIT the <users> block entirely → If a matching public group exists in orgState existingGroups, use <publicGroups> instead Only put values inside <user> tags that look exactly like: firstname.lastname@company.com
OTHER RULES: - <name> is the display label - Queue API name (DeveloperName) has no spaces — use underscores - ONE <queueMembers> block maximum — nest all member types inside it - Inside queueMembers: <users> for users, <publicGroups> for groups, <roles> for roles - <doesIncludeBosses> is NOT a valid Queue element — do not include it - For UPDATE: Start from existingQueues in org state — preserve ALL existing values unless ticket changes them - For DELETE: Use destructiveFiles with metadataType Queue and member = Queue API name
Queue XML format, element order, and member rules
